texto: The most important questions are the ones we can't hope to answer with words. Words are like toys : they amuse and educate us for a time, but when we come to manhood, we should give them up.'Sunmer shakes his head.' The words are all we have,' he says.' If we give them up, we are no better than the beasts.'Otto smiles at Sunmer's wrong headedness.' Then you must find out the explanations on your own, if that is what you truly think.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Ian McGuire: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “The most important questions are the ones we can't hope to answer with words. Words are like toys…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, words, sunmer pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

If we give them up, we are no better than the beasts.'Otto smiles at Sunmer's wrong headedness.' Then you must find out the explanations on your own, if that is what you truly think.

texto: Oh, the others will talk and plan and make oaths and promises, but there are precious few fuckers who will do.
explicacao: Citação de Ian McGuire sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Oh, the others will talk and plan and make oaths and promises, but there are precious few fuckers…” articula reflexao, action, empty-words em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como empty-words e inaction.

texto: The redundancy of flesh, he thinks, the helplessness of meat, how can we conjure spirit from a bone?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Ian McGuire que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “The redundancy of flesh, he thinks, the helplessness of meat, how can we conjure spirit from a bone?”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, flesh, spirit, com foco em spirit.
